posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: Help shape how the world understands AI. We're hiring two designers at Epoch AI to turn complex research into dashboards and visualizations researchers and policymakers can easily use. https://t.co/uDPaX6vGwh

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
How close is AI to automating AI R&D? Right now, the tools economists use to track automation are too blunt to say.

In this week's newsletter, @datagenproc, @joemkwon, and @ansonwhho propose a sharper tool: a thorough taxonomy of 60+ tasks involved in frontier AI research. 🧵 https://t.co/sCRogvjgwL

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: While these companies continue to be profitable, and all but Oracle still have substantial cash reserves, most have already turned to external financing to fund their growing investments in AI infrastructure, e.g., Alphabet's recent $85 billion equity raise.

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
The end of the self-funded AI buildout?

Hyperscaler cash capex is growing much faster than cash inflows. On current trends, they will be unable to fully fund the AI infrastructure buildout with cash from operations by the end of this year. https://t.co/qHcXs4rn93

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: Claude Fable 5 achieves a new high score of 161 on the Epoch Capabilities Index! This beats out GPT-5.5 Pro by 1 point, and is the first time Anthropic has taken the lead on the ECI in over a year. https://t.co/qBArsYoZF1

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: Claude Fable 5 scores very well on FrontierMath: Tiers 1–4 (v2), reaching 87% on Tiers 1–3 and 88% on Tier 4. This continues a streak of Anthropic models improving rapidly at math. https://t.co/tLLRWDoA5o

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
FrontierMath: Tiers 1–4 (v2) is live.

We concluded an audit that addressed errors in 42% of problems. Rankings are similar but scores are higher across the board. The current leaders are GPT-5.5 (xhigh) with 85% on Tiers 1–3 and Google’s AI co-mathematician with 76% on Tier 4. https://t.co/DH9nhpKH0N

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
FrontierMath: Tiers 1–4 (v2) is live.

We concluded an audit that addressed errors in 42% of problems. Rankings are similar but scores are higher across the board. The current leaders are GPT-5.5 (xhigh) with 85% on Tiers 1–3 and Google’s AI co-mathematician with 76% on Tier 4. https://t.co/DH9nhpKH0N

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
How big a leap is Mythos in cyber capabilities?

@timotheechauvin, @AlexBarry4, @js_denain, and @ansonwhho compiled the public evidence and found that while it’s unclear if Mythos was ahead of trend in discovering vulnerabilities, it represents a big jump in exploiting them. 🧵 https://t.co/xxxl4glxv8

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
The record for computing capacity in a single data center has doubled every 7 months.

Colossus 1, Anthropic-Amazon New Carlisle, and Meta Prometheus have each claimed the top spot in turn. https://t.co/YZNbvYbMXX

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
The record for computing capacity in a single data center has doubled every 7 months.

Colossus 1, Anthropic-Amazon New Carlisle, and Meta Prometheus have each claimed the top spot in turn. https://t.co/YZNbvYbMXX

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
How should we think through various proposals for sharing the gains of AGI?

According to @pawtrammell and @ansonwhho, the leading proposals for universal redistribution after AGI differ along a primary axis: how much direct control over capital they propose giving citizens. 🧵 https://t.co/JpbWKxPfZp

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
AI companies say their models are getting better at finding software vulnerabilities. Is that bearing out in public data?

Introducing our Cyber Vulnerabilities explorer, which visualizes Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) reported to the CVE Program since 2022. https://t.co/WxkhIORMtR

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
The AI boom has doubled computing infrastructure's share of US GDP.

Investment in AI-related data center construction, compute hardware, and networking equipment accounted for ~0.8% of US GDP in Q1 2026, driving computing infrastructure as a whole to ~1.5% of GDP. https://t.co/5Qi9PDe6e7

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: We took another look at the capability gap between open-weight and proprietary models. Since the start of the year, open-weight models have lagged the state of the art by four months. https://t.co/Oi3cMpGtx3

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: We took another look at the capability gap between open-weight and proprietary models. Since the start of the year, open-weight models have lagged the state of the art by four months. https://t.co/Oi3cMpGtx3

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
We've added narrations to our long-form content on the Epoch AI website, including reports, Gradient Updates, and topic overviews.

Look for the play button. https://t.co/VdcmkUGXfl

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: Hyperscaler capital expenditures came in on trend in Q1 2026, continuing the trajectory that projects them spending $770 billion this year and over a trillion dollars in 2027. https://t.co/M9SULVukV2

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: Our supply estimate is based on serving Kimi K2.6, a trillion-parameter model with 32B active parameters. Using 8k:1k input-to-output token requests, we estimate it would be possible to serve ~20B output tok/s, enough to serve every person on earth 7M tokens per month.

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Are we nearing a compute crunch?

In our latest Gradient Update, @luke__emberson and @Jsevillamol estimate how many tokens all the Blackwell chips on Earth could serve, and compare this to total token demand. Direct comparisons are difficult, but it appears demand is growing much https://t.co/oaqB0B5Od5

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
For frontier AI chips, memory is the largest and fastest-growing component cost.

High-bandwidth memory (HBM) has grown from 52% to 63% of total AI chip component spending between Q1 2024 and Q4 2025. https://t.co/Ey90W2YhE6

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
For frontier AI chips, memory is the largest and fastest-growing component cost.

High-bandwidth memory (HBM) has grown from 52% to 63% of total AI chip component spending between Q1 2024 and Q4 2025. https://t.co/Ey90W2YhE6

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
OpenAI kicked off the AI compute buildout in 2023. But today it uses ~10% of the world's compute, and the top labs together are probably under half.

In this week's newsletter, @justjoshinyou13 discusses how much that share may change, and when it could hit a ceiling.

🧵 https://t.co/d5ajJbi3Zq

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Claude is typically better at software engineering and worse at math than frontier competitors.

Aggregating benchmarks to create our domain-specific ECI, we find the Claude family has an average SWE-ECI 2.7 points higher than their general ECI, and a Math-ECI 1.8 points lower. https://t.co/J9UrHXTNgq

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Servers account for 60% of the total cost of owning a 1 GW AI data center.

A typical 1 GW AI data center costs about $38B in up-front capital and $0.9B/year to operate. Annualizing the capital expenses over equipment lifespans, that equates to $8.5B/year, with $5B for servers. https://t.co/0Y7teqvr9F

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
We see examples of this dynamic in other fields:

- In the 100m sprint, 1st gets way more reward/recognition than 2nd, despite often finishing neck-and-neck

- Some artists earn far more than others, but it's hard to argue this reflects big differences in quality https://t.co/e9HKPIsEB1

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Superstar AI researchers are paid >10× more than their frontier lab colleagues, and >100× more than most postdocs. Why?

The naive explanation is that this is just due to differences in researcher quality. But in a new essay, @ansonwhho argues that this is very incomplete. https://t.co/5YjTglQckZ

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Superstar AI researchers are paid >10× more than their frontier lab colleagues, and >100× more than most postdocs. Why?

The naive explanation is that this is just due to differences in researcher quality. But in a new essay, @ansonwhho argues that this is very incomplete. https://t.co/5YjTglQckZ

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: We're looking for a Researcher to join Epoch AI's data center tracking project! You'd help us monitor global frontier AI datacenter buildouts in detail—using satellite imagery, permits, and other sources to inform policymakers and the public. https://t.co/tygGKuRY9w

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: We are conducting an AI-assisted review of FrontierMath: Tiers 1-4. This has flagged fatal errors in about a third of problems, and we believe most of these flags to be valid. We will release updated scores on a corrected dataset after completing a thorough human review.

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Anthropic and OpenAI earn more revenue per employee than the top public tech companies, both now and at their IPOs.

Anthropic: ~$9M
OpenAI: ~$5.6M
Top public co. (Nvidia): ~$5.1M https://t.co/iLxOVcsbVJ

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Every AI chip consists of key components. If the supply of any component stalls, so can chip production.

Introducing our AI Chip Components explorer, which tracks supply and consumption of logic, packaging, and memory to identify the bottlenecks shaping frontier AI compute. https://t.co/j5Xs6bNwsz

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: We are launching domain-specific capability scores, tracking the capabilities of models across SWE and Math benchmarks, using the same scale as the general ECI. We also support customization for users who want to create their own variants of the ECI. Link below! https://t.co/H4ICWasd0W

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
The recipe for “classic” reasoning benchmarks is simple: text-only, several-hour time horizons, easy to grade, with expert human baselines.

What next? In this week’s Gradient Update, @GregHBurnham argues it’s as easy as dropping one of these four ingredients. https://t.co/jZSPz3ReH0

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
The recipe for “classic” reasoning benchmarks is simple: text-only, several-hour time horizons, easy to grade, with expert human baselines.

What next? In this week’s Gradient Update, @GregHBurnham argues it’s as easy as dropping one of these four ingredients. https://t.co/jZSPz3ReH0

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Join us for in-person workshops to develop problems for FrontierMath: Open Problems!

We are seeking highly interesting unsolved problems from research mathematics whose solutions can be verified programmatically. These are hard to find. Come take a crack at it! Link below. https://t.co/1RDr8PY2BT

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Are AI benchmarks doomed?

@GregHBurnham and @tmkadamcz join @ansonwhho to push back on benchmark pessimism and dig into what the next generation of AI benchmarks could look like.

(0:00:00) - Preview
(0:00:36) - Intro: Are AI benchmarks doomed?
(0:03:13) - The costs and benefits https://t.co/YjhSr5988z

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: How much AI compute has been smuggled to China? We estimate between 290k and 1.6M H100-equivalents by the end of 2025 — representing ~20% to ~60% of China’s total compute. https://t.co/HFIVlq2itv

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: GPT-5.5 Pro achieves a new high score of 159 on the Epoch Capabilities Index! ECI is our statistical tool that combines multiple benchmarks into a unified scale. https://t.co/ktLKbsp9K3

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Alongside our report on how fast robot production could scale up, we've also released a series of @metaculus forecasting questions on the future of robotics.

If you have views on where these trends are headed, come weigh in! https://t.co/bg4Ab2QilH

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: How fast could production of humanoids, quadrupeds, drones, and other robots scale up, in the event of a large demand shock? https://t.co/7I2IjDukad

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: How fast could production of humanoids, quadrupeds, drones, and other robots scale up, in the event of a large demand shock? https://t.co/7I2IjDukad

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
We now estimate that only about 0.3 GW of total facility power is operational for Stargate Abilene, not 0.6 GW. We have moved the 0.6 GW milestone to late May and the 1.2 GW milestone from Q3 to Q4 2026, but both are uncertain.

More about this change and our methodology in 🧵

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Yesterday, we shared a chart showing 80% of Claude users live in $100k+ households, more than any other major AI service.

But Claude’s user base is smaller than other AI services, so this isn’t the same as being the most popular service among high-income households. https://t.co/QZcQ8CutwB

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
80% of US adults who report using Claude in the previous week live in households earning $100,000 or more a year, compared to 37% of Meta AI users.

Other major providers cluster in a relatively narrow band, with 56–64% of users in $100,000+ households. https://t.co/4PJnbqJB8X

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: Opus 4.7 scores 156 on ECI, our tool for combining multiple benchmarks onto a single scale. This puts it a bit ahead of Opus 4.6 and a bit behind only GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and GPT-5.4 Pro. Thread with individual scores and commentary. https://t.co/naZ3A1Bl3F

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content: Opus 4.7 scores 156 on ECI, our tool for combining multiple benchmarks onto a single scale. This puts it a bit ahead of Opus 4.6 and a bit behind only GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and GPT-5.4 Pro. Thread with individual scores and commentary. https://t.co/naZ3A1Bl3F

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
In 2025, OpenAI announced Stargate, a $500 billion data center initiative. We surveyed all 7 US sites and found visible development at each.

There's a long road ahead, but the project appears on track to reach 9+ GW by 2029—comparable to New York City's peak power demand. 🧵 https://t.co/CRwJmqDnEv

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
Have AI capabilities accelerated?

On 3 out of the 4 AI capability metrics we investigated, we found strong evidence of acceleration, around when reasoning models emerged. https://t.co/YHnsG7UbmP

posts.author: Epoch AI
posts.content:
According to our latest polls, Claude usage in the US rose by over 40% amid increased attention last month, but remains far behind ChatGPT.

Our point estimate would imply several million new weekly users in the United States. https://t.co/EDsBMNy5bi
